You probably have better notes than I do.
And then said, let's just, you know, hold off for now.
But I liked the overall concept.
And so Jason just started about a week ago.

And one of the things I, you know, amongst every other job is, you know, we just need more information out there that AI can suck up, that can hit SEO, that can basically make it so that we pop into every single search out there.
And so I showed Jason, you know, the notes I had taken and, you know, some more info about GrowthX and thought, well, let's, let's...

Perfect.
That sounds great.
So, Jason, it sounds like you do have like some kind of just, you know, just some basic.

You know, growthx level, end-to-end partner, we're going to take on everything with regard to content creation from the initial strategy.
And that's a big part of phase one, our strategy sprint, the two-month engagement.

All the while, you know, we have a platform that we've built AI workflows in so that in phase one, we can get the best context to feed those workflows and create content that's going to be the most relevant to the personas that you're targeting, to the SEO and GEO opportunities that we identify.
And a big, you know, part of the growthx partnership.
Appreciate it.

So we'll take on like the main, we'll take on the main responsibility of everything involved in content creation.

Maybe just...
Super quickly, yeah, I'm not familiar with him.
Yeah, definitely.
So Marcel, his whole philosophy has been since like the early social media days at IBM, brands should think more like publishers, right?

Like why spend so much on paid to rent your audience when you can build a content engine that's going to have a much higher ROI and like a lower, right, customer acquisition cost if you can really get things humming.
And so at IBM, he built a microsite called securityintelligence.com.
It was live for like over 10 years and was their highest ROI marketing channel.
He did something similar at HP, built like a contributor network of over a thousand experts for the site that they had created.
Again and again, he'd like repeated this process of building a content engine to drive organic traffic.
HashiCorp, he joined when they were 6 million.
They scaled above a hundred million.
He was like the first and last CMO at scale.
As he likes to call it, and they, you know, did some things around like a conference where they had Sam Altman speak.
So basically, like he, you know, has lived and breathed this as a marketing leader since his early career.

And at DeepGram, that's when he started, you know, focusing all of his time building these AI workflows that would basically represent all of the messy middle of that process of creating great content, right?
So when you look at like a piece like this, like Augment Code, you know, they're a competitor of Cursor, super competitive space right now.
We've built all these different guides for them.
So a lot of what we do, it's like editorial focused as the starting point, because that's really the foundation that you need to build.
And so if we take this article right here, I can show you like how we created it, right?
Like, we've got a table where it's comparing to different competitors in their space.
There's a lot of cross-linking in the content we create.
It's...
It's really driven by the artifacts first and foremost, because the content's only going to be as good as what we feed, right, into the workflows themselves.
But when you think about, like, this piece in particular, what we're doing here is we're creating a brief around a specific assignment.
We're doing deep research to inform that brief and what is going to be created as an article.
We're going deep on fact-checking, the cross-linking opportunities, generating the SEO meta tags.

And although it, like, looks like seven different steps in this process, the beauty of how we work is that each one of these steps is, like, hundreds of steps, right, where we're connecting to different APIs, where we're leveraging thousands of different tokens to make sure that this is more than just, like, you're going into ChatGPT and you're trying to create content using AI, right?
It's, like, fetching keyword traffic.
Analyzing competitor content, looking at search intent, and then using all of that information to inform the brief that's created.
If we look at the research step, we're generating research questions, and then we're performing deep research on each one of those questions, backing it up by legitimate third-party sources.
And so that's really what you're getting from the GrowthX partnership is someone that can go in and do exactly what you said, Michael.
Create content that's going to help you guys show up in AI answers and also benefit from a traditional SEO perspective as well.

Thank you.

very relevant question, lots of parts there, so I'll give you as much clarity as I can.

in the of that.

demo.
But auto is essentially a corporate travel, you know, AI, think of it less as AI, that's the technology, that's all fun and games.
But it's really about giving every single business traveler their own executive assistant.

And so you can really think of it as someone you can talk to and say, hey, I'm heading to this conference, go find the details of that conference for me, build a whole, you know, give me my flight option, my hotel options.
Auto knows that you prefer United or Delta, it knows that you prefer non-stops, you hate, you know, red eyes, these are the types of hotels you like staying in.
so just like the best ever executive assistant, it's hyper personalized and finds everything you need, and then takes care of service issues along the way.
So flight gets canceled, flight gets moved, your meeting gets moved, you need to move it, you know, auto will automatically handle all those things.
